title: Idempotency model
description: Ansible modules declare desired state and report changed only when they had to act to reach it.
body: Use when reasoning about why repeated runs should be safe. A well-behaved module compares the host's current state to the declared target and only acts (and reports changed) when they differ, so re-running converges without side effects. Raw shell/command tasks break this because they run unconditionally and always report changed. Do not measure success by 'it ran' but by 'it stopped changing'. Actions: prefer modules and guard raw commands. Validation signals include a second run reporting zero changed. Risk boundary: a perpetually-changed task hides real drift and triggers handlers needlessly.
